skill.name: developer-growth-analysis
skill.description: Analyzes your recent Claude Code chat history to identify coding patterns, development gaps, and areas for improvement, curates relevant learning resources from HackerNews, and automatically sends a personalized growth report to your Slack DMs.
skill.summary: Analyzes recent Claude Code chats to produce a personalized developer growth report.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static shell execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples and ordinary prose. The skill still has medium privacy risk because it instructs the assistant to read local Claude Code chat history, including pasted content, and deliver a derived report through Slack and external resource lookups.

medium_findings.title: Local Claude Code Chat History Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read ~/.claude/history.jsonl and analyze message text, project names, timestamps, and pasted content. This can expose sensitive source code, credentials, client data, or private development context if the user does not review the data scope.
medium_findings.reasoning: The hidden home-directory file access is real. It is documented and relevant to the skill purpose, but it creates a meaningful privacy risk because pastedContents may contain sensitive material.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and fields to inspect are explicitly named in the instructions. The risk follows directly from analyzing chat history and pasted content.

medium_findings.title: External Delivery of Derived Private Report
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to send the complete developer growth report to Slack DMs. The report is derived from local chat history and may include sensitive project details or inferred weaknesses.
medium_findings.reasoning: Slack delivery is an intended feature and not hidden, but it moves analysis derived from local development history into an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require Slack connection checks and sending the report as formatted messages. The privacy risk depends on report content but is clearly present.

medium_findings.title: External Learning Resource Search
medium_findings.description: The skill uses Rube MCP to search HackerNews for resources based on inferred improvement areas. Search queries may reveal details about the user's technologies, projects, or skill gaps to an external service.
medium_findings.reasoning: External search is part of the documented workflow. It should be treated as a disclosure channel because queries are generated from private chat analysis.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external search workflow is explicit, but the exact query content depends on runtime analysis. The risk is likely but bounded by implementation behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and inline examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No Ruby code, shell command substitution, or executable script is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown examples showing prompts or report output. They do not instruct the assistant to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows fenced Markdown blocks and plain text examples. There is no executable language context or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the reviewed lines contain prose such as descriptions, example report text, dates, and links. No cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.reasoning: No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use appears in the skill. The scanner likely matched substrings inside ordinary words.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are natural-language documentation and output examples. There is no code or instruction to perform cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a line about identifying user challenges as system reconnaissance. The text describes analyzing development problems in chat history, not collecting system information.
low_findings.reasoning: The line asks the assistant to look for repeated questions and knowledge gaps. It does not request hostnames, users, processes, network data, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is clearly within a developer coaching workflow. No system discovery commands or host enumeration instructions are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Home Directory File Read
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow reads ~/.claude/history.jsonl from the user's home directory. This is sensitive local data access and should require clear user consent.
dangerous_patterns.reasoning: The hidden file access is explicit and directly tied to the skill behavior. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a sensitive filesystem pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact path and expected data fields are named. The security impact is privacy exposure, not command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Private Analysis Sent to External Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow sends a complete report through Slack and searches HackerNews through Rube MCP. These external interactions may reveal private development context.
dangerous_patterns.reasoning: External service usage is documented and useful, but it creates a disclosure path from local chat history to network-connected tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions name Rube MCP, HackerNews search, Slack connection management, and Slack message delivery. The exact payload depends on runtime report content.

content.user_title: Analyze Developer Growth From Recent Chats
content.value_statement: Developers often miss patterns in their own coding work and repeat the same learning gaps. This skill reviews recent Claude Code history, identifies improvement areas, and prepares a focused growth report with learning resources.
actual_capabilities: Reads recent local Claude Code chat history when available.
Identifies projects, technologies, problem types, and repeated challenges.
Creates a prioritized developer growth report with strengths and action items.
Searches HackerNews through Rube MCP for learning resources tied to improvement areas.
Formats the report for Slack delivery to the user's direct messages.
limitations: Requires access to local Claude Code history at the expected path.
May miss work done outside Claude Code or outside the selected time window.
Learning resources depend on HackerNews search quality and availability.
Slack delivery requires an active and authorized Slack connection.

use_cases.title: Weekly Growth Review
use_cases.description: Review recent coding conversations and turn recurring struggles into a focused learning plan.

use_cases.title: Mentor Preparation
use_cases.description: Summarize current strengths, blockers, and study topics before a coaching session.

use_cases.title: Skill Planning
use_cases.description: Map active project work to specific resources for TypeScript, React, DevOps, or backend improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Growth Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with the default recent history window.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my developer growth from my recent Claude Code chats.

prompt_templates.title: Today Only
prompt_templates.scenario: Limit analysis to a short work session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my work from today and identify the top three improvement areas.

prompt_templates.title: Project Focus
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus feedback on one project or technical area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my recent chats about the current project and recommend targeted learning resources.

prompt_templates.title: Mentor Ready Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a structured report for external feedback.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a developer growth report from the last 48 hours with evidence, priorities, action items, and resources I can discuss with a mentor.

output_examples.input: Analyze my developer growth from my recent chats.
output_examples.output: A report summarizing recent projects, key technologies, strengths, three prioritized improvement areas, action items, and learning resources.

output_examples.input: Analyze my work from today and suggest areas for improvement.
output_examples.output: A focused daily review with observed challenges, practical recommendations, estimated study time, and Slack-ready sections.

output_examples.input: Find resources for the improvement areas you identify.
output_examples.output: A curated reading list grouped by improvement area, with short explanations of why each resource is relevant.

best_practices: Review the report before sending it to Slack if chats may contain sensitive information.
Run the analysis on a consistent weekly cadence to compare progress over time.
Act on one improvement area at a time instead of treating every recommendation as urgent.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill on chat history that contains secrets or confidential client data without cleanup.
Do not treat inferred skill gaps as performance ratings or formal evaluations.
Do not send reports to shared Slack channels unless the user explicitly requests it.

faq.question: What data does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes recent Claude Code chat history, including messages, project names, timestamps, and pasted content when present.

faq.question: Does it require Slack?
faq.answer: Slack is used for delivery, but the report can still be reviewed in the assistant response before sending.

faq.question: Can it analyze any time range?
faq.answer: The instructions focus on the past 24 to 48 hours, but users can ask for a specific recent period.

faq.question: Are the learning resources personalized?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill searches for resources based on improvement areas inferred from the user's recent work.

faq.question: Is this a security review tool?
faq.answer: No. It may notice security-related learning needs, but it is designed for developer coaching and growth planning.

faq.question: What should users check before running it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm that their chat history does not include secrets, private client data, or content they do not want summarized.
